title: Autonomous-mode: config + checkpoint bypass, wired into tk next
description:
Add global autonomous-mode and the checkpoint-bypass it enables. Builds on V1.

1. internal/config: add an autonomous-mode setting (e.g. Policy.AutonomousMode *bool, matching the existing *bool option style) + a resolve helper defaulting to false.
2. Selection gating: in the planning/ready selection path (plan.go needsPlanning and/or ready.go), when autonomous-mode is enabled, treat awaiting type 'checkpoint' as NON-gating (project boundaries flow through) while keeping all other awaiting types gating. Default (off) behavior is unchanged — this is the key backwards-compat point.
3. tk next wiring: thread the resolved mode into runNext; add a way to enable it for a run (a --autonomous flag and/or reading the config). 

Per-project auto-continue and the project close-out tick are CONVENTIONS handled by the skill docs (af5), not here — this tick only provides the global switch + the bypass mechanism. Design: §5, §8 (the two checkpoint knobs).

Files: internal/config/config.go, internal/query/plan.go (and/or ready.go), cmd/tk/cmd/next.go + tests.
acceptance_criteria: go test -short ./internal/config/... ./internal/query/... ./cmd/... passes; with autonomous-mode OFF a project-checkpoint (awaiting:checkpoint) gates continuation as today; with it ON, checkpoint-type awaits no longer gate planning while OTHER awaiting types still do; tk next exposes the mode
